3.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.

4.why: The walk fetches nodes by id.
4.invariant: p_by[i] is p's node at slot i.

5.why: We loop over each slot to build nodes.

6.happening: Start p's child-slot walker just past the root.
6.why: Children are handed out in array order.
6.invariant: pc is p's next unassigned slot.

7.happening: Loop over p's slots to build nodes.
7.why: Each real value becomes a node.

9.happening: Create p node {i} with empty child links.
9.why: Children are filled from later slots.
9.invariant: A fresh node has no children.

10.happening: Is a slot left for a left child?
10.why: Children come from the next slots.

11.happening: Is that slot a real node?

12.happening: Link slot {pc} as node {i}'s left child.
12.invariant: left now points at a child.

14.happening: Is a slot left for a right child?
14.why: The right child follows the left.

15.happening: Is that slot a real node?

16.happening: Link slot {pc} as node {i}'s right child.
16.invariant: right now points at a child.

18.happening: Add the finished node to p.
18.why: It has its value and children.

19.why: For fast lookup during the walk.

20.why: Same build, for the second tree.

21.why: The walk fetches q's nodes by id.
21.invariant: q_by[i] is q's node at slot i.

22.why: We loop over each slot to build nodes.

23.happening: Start q's child-slot walker just past the root.
23.why: Children are handed out in array order.
23.invariant: qc is q's next unassigned slot.

24.happening: Loop over q's slots to build nodes.
24.why: Each real value becomes a node.

26.happening: Create q node {i} with empty child links.
26.why: Children are filled from later slots.
26.invariant: A fresh node has no children.

27.happening: Is a slot left for a left child?
27.why: Children come from the next slots.

28.happening: Is that slot a real node?

29.happening: Link slot {qc} as node {i}'s left child.
29.invariant: left now points at a child.

31.happening: Is a slot left for a right child?
31.why: The right child follows the left.

32.happening: Is that slot a real node?

33.happening: Link slot {qc} as node {i}'s right child.
33.invariant: right now points at a child.

35.happening: Add the finished node to q.
35.why: It has its value and children.

36.why: For fast lookup during the walk.

37.happening: Assume the trees are the same to start.
37.why: We only flip this on a real difference.

38.happening: done is False — the walk is still going.
38.why: It flips true on the first difference.

40.why: It drives the walk over p.
40.invariant: stack_p holds p positions to compare.

41.invariant: stack_q holds the matching q positions.

42.why: The comparison starts at the roots.
42.invariant: stack_p starts with p's root or None.

43.invariant: stack_q starts with q's root or None.

44.why: No position is being compared yet.
44.invariant: cur_p is the current p position.

45.why: No position is being compared yet.
45.invariant: cur_q is the current q position.

46.happening: Positions remain and no difference yet — compare the next pair.
46.why: We walk both trees in lockstep.

46.happening: The walk is over — either all matched or a difference stopped it.
46.why: No positions left, or done was set.

47.why: This is the p node to compare now.
47.invariant: cur_p is the current p position.

48.invariant: cur_p and cur_q are the same location.

49.happening: Both positions are empty — they agree here.
At least one node exists here.
49.invariant: Fall through to the structure/value checks.

50.why: Two nulls are a valid match.

51.happening: Exactly one side is empty — the structures differ!
51.why: One tree has a node where the other has a gap.
51.invariant: The trees are not the same.

51.happening: Both nodes exist — compare their values next.

52.happening: Mark the trees as NOT the same.

53.why: One difference is enough to decide.

54.happening: Both nodes exist but their values differ ({p_by[cur_p]["value"]} vs {q_by[cur_q]["value"]}).
54.why: Same tree needs equal values at every position.
54.invariant: The trees are not the same.

54.happening: The two values are equal — this position matches.
54.why: Structure and value both agree here.

55.happening: Mark the trees as NOT the same.

56.why: One difference is enough to decide.

58.why: This position agrees in both trees.

60.happening: Queue q's left child alongside it.
60.why: Compare the same position in q.

62.happening: Queue q's right child alongside it.
62.why: Compare the same position in q.

63.happening: Return whether the trees are the same: {same}.
63.why: Every position agreed, or a difference was found.

init.happening: Building both trees and seeding the paired stacks with the roots.
init.why: We need node objects before comparing.
init.invariant: p on top, q below; roots paired.

loop.happening: Popping the next matching position from both stacks.
loop.why: We walk both trees in lockstep.

move.happening: Landing the two cursors on the corresponding nodes.
move.why: Each pop is one position in both trees.
move.invariant: cur_p and cur_q are the same position.

check.why: Same tree needs same structure and values.

update.happening: Recording a match and queueing the children of both.
update.invariant: Children are pushed in the same order.

return.happening: Returning whether the trees are the same.
return.why: Either all positions matched or one differed.
